questions.question: What is the primary mission of the DAF Emergency Management (EM) Program?
options: To coordinate with host nation authorities for disaster relief operations
To prevent, protect, prepare for, respond to, and recover from all hazards/threats that pose the greatest risk to lives, key resources, facilities, infrastructure, and operational missions
To establish MOPP levels for all DAF installations worldwide
To train first responders in CBRN defense techniques

questions.question: How many pillars of EM preparedness does the DAF EM Program accomplish its missions through?
Which of the following is NOT one of the seven pillars of EM preparedness?
The Crisis Action Team (CAT) is intended to focus on what primary function?
options: Management of the incident at the scene

questions.question: Which EM mission area is defined as 'activities that address the short-term, direct effects of an incident'?
What does the Emergency Communications Center (ECC) provide?
options: Strategic-level planning for CBRN threats only
A 24/7 operation incorporating core functions of fire and emergency services, security forces, and medical dispatch
Weekly briefings on installation emergency status
Coordination between PAR teams and the installation commander

questions.question: Due to their persistent nature, which two types of chemical agents pose the primary threat to air bases?
Which statement about biological agents is correct?
options: Symptoms of biological attacks are always experienced immediately after contact
Biological agents can only be found as dry powders
Symptoms of biological attacks may not be experienced immediately after contact; each agent has a different incubation period
Biological agents only include viruses and bacteria

questions.question: What is the most important action in the event of a toxic industrial material incident?
options: Don the protective mask and remain in place
Report the incident to the CBRN Control Center before taking any action
Immediately evacuate to an area outside the hazard's path, if feasible

questions.question: What does a three-to-five-minute wavering tone indicate?
options: A disaster or incident is imminent or in progress
An attack or hostile act is imminent or in progress
An active shooter incident is in progress
The all clear has been sounded

questions.question: What does the announcement 'Lockdown, Lockdown, Lockdown' indicate?
options: A natural disaster warning requiring shelter-in-place
An active shooter incident is in progress
An air or ground attack is imminent

questions.question: During Alarm Yellow, what MOPP level is directed (or as directed)?
What does Alarm Yellow indicate?
options: Attack is probable in less than 30 minutes
Air or ground attack is imminent or in progress
Attack is over and CBRN contamination is suspected

questions.question: During Alarm Black, what type of release allows Post Attack Reconnaissance teams to deploy?
What is the purpose of Split MOPP?
options: To allow individual units to choose their own MOPP level
To divide an installation into two or more CBRN zones rather than declaring the entire installation contaminated
To alternate MOPP levels between day and night shifts
To reduce the required MOPP level by half during low-threat conditions

questions.question: What is the best immediate protective equipment against chemical agents regardless of agent type, concentration, or method of attack?
options: The protective mask with filters only

questions.question: At MOPP Level Two, which items are worn and which are carried?
options: Wear: Overgarment and Footwear; Carry: Mask/Hood and Handwear
Wear: Field Gear, Overgarment, and Footwear; Carry: Mask/Hood and Handwear
Wear: Field Gear and Overgarment; Carry: Footwear and Mask/Hood

questions.question: What is the purpose of Collective Protection Systems (COLPRO)?
options: To provide individual chemical agent detection for each unit member
To provide protection from CBRN agents by utilizing an overpressure system to keep threat agents outside certain facilities
To store protective equipment for rapid deployment
To decontaminate personnel after exposure to chemical agents

questions.question: When covering equipment that cannot be placed under overhead cover, how many layers of barrier material should be used on tops of vehicles and equipment when possible?
What reporting method is used as a quick and effective way to communicate attack information?
In the SALUTE reporting method, what does the 'U' stand for?
What are the most effective methods of removing chemical agents from skin or protective equipment?
options: Reactive skin decontamination lotion and M295 individual decontamination kits
Soap and water followed by M8 detector paper verification
0.5-percent chlorine solution and M9 detector paper

questions.question: Why should chlorine solutions for decontamination only be used after consultation with CBRN Defense Specialists?
options: Chlorine solutions are only effective against biological agents
Some chemicals react negatively to chlorine
Chlorine solutions damage protective equipment beyond repair
Chlorine solutions are only available in limited quantities

questions.question: What is a Class A mishap?
options: Costs totaling $600,000 or more but less than $2,500,000
Costs totaling $60,000 or more but less than $600,000
Costs totaling $2,500,000, destruction of a DoD aircraft, or a fatality or permanent total disability
Costs totaling $25,000 or more but less than $60,000

questions.question: Within how many hours must individuals report personal injury, property damage, or suspected exposure to hazardous materials?
How many days do investigators have to respond to hazard reports in writing?
How many principles govern all actions associated with risk management?
What are the five steps of the Risk Management Process in order?
options: Identify Hazards, Assess Hazards, Develop Controls and Make Decisions, Implement Controls, Supervise and Evaluate
Assess Hazards, Identify Hazards, Make Decisions, Implement Controls, Review and Report
Identify Risks, Analyze Risks, Develop Countermeasures, Deploy Countermeasures, Monitor Results

questions.question: In Real-Time Risk Management, what are the three stages of situational awareness required for a complete assessment?
options: Perception of what is happening, Integration of information and goals, and Projection into the future

questions.question: What are the 'Knock-it-off' and 'Timeout' concepts integral to?
